item.title: Artemis crew tells kids moon flight was "the best roller coaster ride"
item.description: The Artemis II crew joined "CBS Mornings" Friday for a live town hall where they took questions from kids just weeks after returning from their historic moon mission.

item.title: What are today's mortgage interest rates: May 1, 2026?
item.description: Want to buy a home or refinance the one you live in now? Here are the mortgage interest rates you'll need to know.

item.title: Artemis II crew reveals what snack they ate right after splashdown
item.description: The Artemis II astronauts said they actually really enjoyed the space food, but it was a familiar candy they enjoyed after splashing down in the Pacific Ocean.

item.title: Trump administration reaches key deadline on Iran war
item.description: The Trump administration has reached the 60-day mark since the Iran war erupted, which means historically Congress should weigh in on the conflict. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi and Ramy Inocencio report.

item.title: Artemis II astronauts talk mission toilet troubles: "We made do"
item.description: The plumbing issues aboard the Orion capsule became headline news in the early days of the historic Artemis II mission.

item.title: Meet the horses running in the Kentucky Derby
item.description: The Kentucky Derby will see a full field of 20 horses in the first leg of the 2026 competition for horse racing's Triple Crown.

item.title: Iran says it has offered a new proposal for peace talks with the U.S.
item.description: Iran says it's offered a new proposal for talks with the U.S. to end the war, as the Strait of Hormuz standoff sends costs soaring around the world.

item.title: Artemis II crew addresses toilet malfunction during spaceflight
item.description: The Artemis II crew addressed on "CBS Mornings" the issues they had with the toilet during their historic spaceflight. Astronaut Victor Glover explained the toilet actually didn't fail, saying "it was the venting system that emptied the tank."

item.title: New details and timeline of Cole Allen encounter at White House Correspondents' Dinner
item.description: New video shows how Cole Allen appears to rush the security checkpoint at the White House Correspondents' Dinner. This comes as more information about his movements emerges. CBS News' Katrina Kaufman reports.

item.title: As Iran war hits key 60-day deadline, Congress and Trump face choices
item.description: The War Powers Resolution sets deadlines for the president to end hostilities without congressional approval.

item.title: Astronaut Jeremy Hansen says "it's not that we never fail" but "we never stop there"
item.description: Artemis II astronaut Jeremy Hansen talks about what the "Apollo 13" movie meant to him and its message about failure and resilience.

item.title: Home prices are dropping in one-third of U.S. cities. Here's where.
item.description: Cities in Florida and California, where home prices soared during the pandemic, saw some of the steepest declines in property values.

item.title: "Apollo 13" director Ron Howard to Artemis crew: "What you accomplished was extraordinary"
item.description: Ron Howard, director of "Apollo 13," which retells the dramatic true story of the aborted 1970 lunar mission, joins the Artemis II crew and "CBS Mornings" to discuss the recent mission.

item.title: Artemis astronauts asked about most memorable moment, toughest decision made on moon mission
item.description: Artemis II astronauts, speaking to an audience of students, took questions about teamwork, the most memorable moment they would like to experience again and the toughest decisions they had to make during the historic moon mission.

item.title: Amtrak may ease rules on guns on its trains, sources say
item.description: Amtrak may ease rules on guns on its trains, sources say. Critics worry that would weaken security even though, authorities say, the accused correspondents' dinner shooter took Amtrak cross-country with his firearms.

item.title: 5-year-old aspiring astronaut Jack says "it was fun" to watch historic Artemis launch
item.description: Jack, a 5-year-old aspiring astronaut, talks to "CBS Mornings" about watching the Artemis II launch in person in Florida.

item.title: Artemis astronauts describe "spectacular" moon mission, eating peanut M&M's after splashdown
item.description: Artemis II astronauts joined "CBS Mornings" for a town hall, "Artemis II: A Celebration of Heroes." They described the moments before liftoff and splashdown, and the funny moment just after coming back to Earth that prompted "CBS Mornings" co-host to say, "astronauts, they're just like us."

item.title: Jewish Londoners describe "utter horror" and living in fear after latest attack
item.description: "We've been warning about this for a long time," one local resident told CBS News. "It's like a tsunami — you see the smaller waves before the big one hits."

item.title: Artemis commander Reid Wiseman describes "dramatic and important warning" while on mission
item.description: Commander Reid Wiseman revealed the most difficult decision the crew had to make while in space on the Artemis II mission. Wiseman described a "dramatic and important warning" that came on in the middle of the night.

item.title: U.K. raises terrorism threat level to "severe" after stabbing attack
item.description: The U.K. has raised its national threat level from "substantial" to "severe," citing the increasing threat of Islamist and extreme right-wing terrorism in the country.

item.title: Artemis astronaut Jeremy Hansen on what he missed about gravity: "Nothing"
item.description: When asked what was his favorite thing about gravity that he missed while in space, Artemis astronaut and mission specialist Jeremy Hansen laughingly said "nothing" and added while microgravity is harder in some aspects "it's just so worth it."

item.title: Artemis' Christina Koch says real heroes are "people on the ground" working on mission
item.description: Artemis astronaut Christina Koch said "teamwork is 100% everything" when it comes to the success of the moon mission. She called out her teammates "on the ground" as the "real heroes."

item.title: Artemis' Victor Glover says seeing Earth in "one glance" is "very special perspective"
item.description: Astronaut Victor Glover said one moment he wishes everyone could experience from the Artemis mission is seeing "all of Earth in one glance."

item.title: Artemis astronauts on readjusting to gravity, capturing photos from space: "It's spectacular"
item.description: Artemis astronaut Christina Koch recalled readjusting her body to Earth's gravity after returning from space, while Reid Wiseman recalled capturing photos from their trip to the moon. "It's spectacular," Wiseman told "CBS Mornings."

item.title: Artemis astronaut Victor Glover describes his thoughts in final seconds before liftoff
item.description: Pilot Victor Glover told "CBS Mornings" what it was like in the seconds before the Artemis II lifted off for their historic mission, saying "we're focused" while adding "you also want to be prepared in case the weather is bad or there is a technical malfunction and you're not going to go that day."

item.title: Feds have rejected 15% of businesses' tariff refund claims
item.description: But after some early hiccups, the U.S. government's hub for businesses seeking tariff refunds is running smoothly, an expert says.

item.title: Could the FCC really yank ABC's TV licenses amid Trump spat with Kimmel?
item.description: Federal telecom regulators can revoke broadcast licenses, but legal experts say the FCC would face a tough road in forcing ABC to go dark.

item.title: New White House drug abuse strategy floats wastewater testing, AI, more treatment
item.description: The Trump administration is proposing wastewater testing to try to ferret out data on illegal drug use in real time, according to a draft of a new drug control strategy obtained by CBS News. It also proposes using AI to track threats.

item.title: Sovereignty wins the 151st running of the Kentucky Derby
item.description: Sovereignty defeated a field of 18 challengers to win the 151st Kentucky Derby Saturday at Churchill Downs.

item.title: Meet the horses who ran in the Kentucky Derby
item.description: The Kentucky Derby started the 2025 competition for horse racing's Triple Crown with a field of 19 horses in the annual Run for the Roses.
